evals.prompt: I'm getting this when I run helm template on my chart: `Error: template: mychart/templates/deployment.yaml:18:25: executing "mychart/templates/deployment.yaml" at <.Values.resources>: nil pointer evaluating interface {}.limits`. The deployment has `resources:\n  {{ toYaml .Values.resources.limits }}` and resources isn't always set. How do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Explain the nil-pointer is because .Values.resources is unset so .limits can't be evaluated. Fix with a guard: `{{- with .Values.resources }}resources:\n  {{- toYaml . | nindent 2 }}{{- end }}`, or default/hasKey. Note toYaml should use nindent for correct indentation, and recommend rendering with helm template to verify. May mention adding it to values.yaml/schema.

evals.prompt: Create a production-ready Helm chart for a stateless REST API called 'orders-api'. It needs a Deployment, Service, configurable image/replicas/resources, liveness/readiness probes, and should follow Helm best practices.
evals.expected_output: Recommend `helm create` as the starting point, then a chart with apiVersion v2, namespaced _helpers.tpl labels/selectorLabels, Deployment using include+nindent for labels and toYaml+nindent for resources/probes, configurable image (tag defaulting to appVersion not latest), replicaCount, resources requests/limits, readiness/liveness probes, a Service, securityContext, and a values.yaml + ideally values.schema.json. Mention helm lint --strict / helm template to validate.

evals.prompt: My `helm upgrade` failed halfway and now `helm list` shows the release as 'pending-upgrade'. Every new `helm upgrade` says another operation is in progress. How do I recover without deleting everything?
evals.expected_output: Explain pending-upgrade means a prior operation was interrupted and never reached a terminal state. Recommend `helm history` to find the last good revision, then `helm rollback <release> <rev>` to clear it. Suggest using --atomic on future upgrades. As a last resort mention the release-state Secret (sh.helm.release.v1.*) can be removed but that's destructive metadata surgery — back up first, prefer rollback.

evals.prompt: I need to deploy PostgreSQL to my cluster. I don't want to write a chart from scratch. How do I find a good existing Helm chart, see what I can configure, and install it pinned to a specific version?
evals.expected_output: Show `helm search hub postgresql` (Artifact Hub) or a known repo; `helm repo add` + `helm repo update`; `helm show values <repo>/postgresql --version X` to see configuration (pipe to a values file); then `helm install <name> <repo>/postgresql --version X -f my-values.yaml`. Mention OCI alternative (oci:// path, no repo add) and checking maintenance/kubeVersion. Pin the version.

Identify: `template` can't be piped so labels indentation will be wrong — use `{{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}`; the tag is unquoted (YAML coercion risk) — quote it; image:tag built by string concat should use quote and ideally default the tag to appVersion not arbitrary; name/replicas should be nil-safe / come from helpers; recommend rendering with helm template and running helm lint --strict. May note label namespacing and that template names are global.
